title: A resolution condemning Beijing's destruction of Hong Kong's democracy and rule of law.
plain_summary: This Senate resolution strongly criticizes China for passing laws that have destroyed democracy in Hong Kong. The resolution condemns China's national security laws that are being used to arrest and jail peaceful protesters and activists who support democracy. It supports the people of Hong Kong who are trying to keep their basic rights and freedoms. The resolution also calls for the Hong Kong government to drop all charges against democracy supporters and free them from jail, including a well-known activist named Jimmy Lai.
last_action_text: Placed on Senate Legislative Calendar under General Orders. Calendar No. 57.
subjects: Rule of law and government transparency

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This is a resolution that condemns Beijing's destruction of Hong Kong's democracy and rule of law.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: In nineteen ninety seven, Great Britain gave Hong Kong back to China. China promised that Hong Kong would be a Special Administrative Region under the one country two systems rule. This meant Hong Kong's Basic Law would protect the basic rights of Hong Kong people. These rights included an independent court system, the right to vote, and freedoms to gather and speak freely. However, the Government of the People's Republic of China has repeatedly weakened Hong Kong's independence since nineteen ninety seven. This led to political protests in Hong Kong, including the two thousand fourteen Umbrella Movement and the two thousand nineteen to two thousand twenty protests. The Hong Kong Police Force used too much force against the protesters, many of whom were under thirty years old. In response to these protests, the Government of the People's Republic of China passed the Hong Kong national security law in June two thousand twenty. This law is poorly written and too broad. It punishes people for using their basic rights and freedoms. The law has been used to crack down on peaceful expression, including freedoms to gather, speak, and practice religion that were promised to Hong Kong under the Basic Law. China also replaced Hong Kong's legislature with people loyal to the Chinese Communist Party. In March two thousand twenty four, Hong Kong passed more national security laws called the Article Twenty Three Ordinance. These laws created more broadly defined crimes including external interference and sabotage. They weakened legal protections for people accused of national security crimes and allowed punishment of Hong Kong citizens and non citizens living overseas. Nearly three hundred people have been arrested under these national security laws. China uses these laws to harass and threaten people who are not Hong Kong citizens and people living outside Hong Kong. China has put cash rewards on democracy activists living in exile outside Hong Kong. On November nineteenth two thousand twenty four, Hong Kong sentenced a group of pro democracy activists, journalists, and former lawmakers known as the Hong Kong Forty Seven to jail terms between four and ten years. This showed Hong Kong's willingness to intimidate and punish its political opponents. Jimmy Lai is a seventy seven year old Hong Kong pro democracy advocate and media owner who has been targeted for decades. He has faced multiple prosecutions for using his rights to peaceful assembly and free speech. He was sentenced to over five years in prison on politically motivated fraud charges. Hong Kong authorities seized his multimillion dollar independent media company Apple Daily. Jimmy Lai has been in prison in solitary confinement with poor medical treatment since December thirty first two thousand twenty. Five Special Rapporteurs and the United Nations Human Rights Council Working Group have found that Jimmy Lai is unlawfully and wrongly detained. They have called for his immediate and unconditional release. Jimmy Lai's trial began on December eighteenth two thousand twenty three but has been delayed repeatedly. International legal experts at the United Nations have raised concerns about prosecutors using witness testimony that may have been obtained through torture, Hong Kong authorities interfering with court independence, and harassment of Jimmy Lai's lawyers. China's weakening of democracy in Hong Kong affects the international order, including the future of Taiwan. Hong Kong has run a public relations campaign to convince global business leaders that Hong Kong remains an important international financial center, while at the same time weakening the independent institutions that helped it grow. Hong Kong has increasingly become a center for moving export controlled goods and helping countries avoid sanctions related to China, North Korea, Russia, and Iran. This directly supports Russia's defense industry and helps its continuing war against Ukraine. Hong Kong still has separate voting power from China at many international organizations including the Asia Pacific Economic Cooperation forum, the Financial Action Task Force, the International Olympic Committee, and the World Trade Organization. This effectively doubles China's voting power at these important institutions. The Hong Kong Human Rights and Democracy Act was signed into law in November two thousand nineteen. It requires the President to impose sanctions to hold accountable those responsible for conduct that undermines basic freedoms and independence in Hong Kong.

sections.title: Senate Resolutions
sections.text: Therefore, the Senate condemns the Government of the People's Republic of China's Hong Kong national security law, Hong Kong's Safeguarding National Security Ordinance, and related violations of internationally recognized human rights. The Senate urges all governments that value democracy or independence to hold the Chinese Communist Party and Hong Kong authorities accountable for destroying Hong Kong's independence, rule of law, and freedoms. The Senate supports the people of Hong Kong as they fight to exercise basic rights and freedoms as listed in the Joint Declaration between the United Kingdom and China on Hong Kong from December nineteenth nineteen eighty four, the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights from December nineteenth nineteen sixty six, and the Universal Declaration of Human Rights from December tenth nineteen forty eight. The Senate condemns the Government of the People's Republic of China's practice of bringing false and politically motivated charges against Hong Kong people and expanding Hong Kong's national security system that destroys the rule of law and undermines citizens' rights in Hong Kong. The Senate calls upon the Hong Kong government to immediately drop all sedition, national security law, and Article Twenty Three related charges and free all defendants immediately, including Jimmy Lai. The Senate expresses extreme concern about the Government of the People's Republic of China's state directed theft of Apple Daily and believes that Hong Kong no longer has credibility as an international business center due to the weakening of the regulatory, legal, and court systems that promoted its economic growth for decades. The Senate encourages the United States Government and other governments to take steps at international institutions to ensure that voting procedures recognize that there is no longer a meaningful difference between Hong Kong and mainland China. The Senate urges the United States Government to use all available and appropriate tools, including those authorized by the Hong Kong Human Rights and Democracy Act, in response to the Government of the People's Republic of China's actions in Hong Kong.
